title: Modern Typing, Generics, and API Evolution · Changed-Input Lab
summary: Type-check interchangeable repositories, policies, events, and domain collections in Python 3.14. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a statically checked domain API with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use Python 3.14 annotation syntax, built-in generics, unions, aliases, and forward references consistently.
Define generic classes and functions whose type parameters express a real input-output relationship.
Reason about covariance, contravariance, invariance, mutation, and substitutability in generic APIs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-oop cumulative stakeholder program."""

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

# Add one tested, typed, inspectable program increment per activity.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: python generics typing. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Oop Modern Annotations, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a statically checked domain API while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Annotations are eagerly evaluated and runtime-enforced in every Python 3.14 program. This fails the Oop Modern Annotations contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
